title: Argyreia hylophila
intro: Argyreia hylophila, commonly known as the Forest Vine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Convolvulaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Argyreia hylophila include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Argyreia hylophila is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make herbal teas, capsules, and tinctures.
